name: Pumpkin Walnut Phyllo"snails
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup olive oil or butter, melted
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the pumpkin in half and remove the seeds and peel using a sharp knife.
2. Grate the pumpkin on medium holes of a cheese grater (approximately 4 cups finely grated).
3. Taste for flavor; add more sugar if desired.
4. Roll up the phyllo "snails":
- Lay each sheet flat, covered with a damp towel except when rolling.
- Brush one filo lightly with oil or butter and fold in half lengthwise.
- Spread about 3 to 4 tablespoons of filling across one end, leaving an inch from each edge.
- Roll up to seal the filling into a long tube.
- Curl tightly into a "snail-like" coil at one end, then set on a baking sheet.
5. Repeat with remaining filo and filling, keeping rolls covered with waxed paper or plastic to avoid drying.
6. Brush rolls with oil or butter and bake in a 350°F oven for 25 minutes or until puffy and crisp.
7. Serve warm or cold with poultry or game dishes.
